# GIGI Builds

> Companion site for *GIGI Builds — The Fiber-Bundle Database: A Working
> Engineer's Guide from Schema to Shard* (Bee Rosa Davis, 2026). GIGI
> (Geometric Intrinsic Global Index) is an open-source database engine,
> written in Rust, that stores records as sections of a fiber bundle and
> computes differential-geometric quantities — curvature, spectral gap,
> holonomy, confidence — as a side effect of ordinary inserts and queries.
> This site carries all 126 of the book's exercises as interactive pages
> plus six "web extra" 3D demos that are not in the printed book. A public
> read-only GIGI instance is live at https://gigi-stream.fly.dev for
> anyone (human or LLM agent) to query.

Everything on this site is client-side HTML+JS; the text content of every
page is readable without executing JavaScript, and the file
[llms-full.txt](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/llms-full.txt)
contains the whole story — engine concepts, GQL reference, live-API usage,
chapter map, and a plain-text explanation of what each interactive demo
computes — in one markdown document. Read that first.

## Docs

- [llms-full.txt](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/llms-full.txt): the complete LLM-readable guide to GIGI, the book, the exercises, the demos, and the live API
- [GIGI engine repository](https://github.com/nurdymuny/gigi): Rust source, GQL reference (GQL_REFERENCE.md), tetmesh spec (GIGI_TETMESH_SPEC_v0.6.md), example harnesses
- [Site front page](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/): the full chapter catalog and all web extras

## Engine surface shipped 2026-07-01 → 2026-07-03

Full grammar and exact error strings are in llms-full.txt §2–3.

- Lattice-gauge topology verbs: `LATTICE ... FROM CUBIC L=24 DIM=4 OBC AXIS 3` (single-axis open boundary — wrap edges omitted), `INGEST ... FORMAT NPZ KEY <name> AS GAUGE_FIELD GROUP SU2|SU3|U1 ON LATTICE l`, `CHERN_CLASS ... ORDER 2`, `SPECTRAL_GAUGE` with WHERE sector stratification, `ALTER BUNDLE ... ADD BASE` (append-only schema evolution)
- Data round trip: `INGEST FORMAT CSV|JSONL|NPZ` in, `EMIT CSV TO 'file.csv'` out — both fail-closed behind containment gates (`GIGI_INGEST_DIR` / `GIGI_EMIT_DIR`; unset means disabled, and paths cannot escape the configured root)
- Query surface: `WITH JACKKNIFE ALONG <field> [SKIP FIRST n]` autocorrelation-honest error bars, `EXPLAIN SECTION` per-field κ rows carrying `record_kappa`, `SHOW FIELDS ON b`, TIMESTAMP fields with ISO-8601 literals and `NOW`
- Ergonomics: human parse errors (token names, near-context, did-you-mean), trailing-token rejection, notice responses for no-op statements, poison-proof locks, and a `gigi` CLI (rustyline REPL, `doctor` health report, `-f script.gql`)

## Engine surface shipped 2026-07-05 → 2026-07-17

Full grammar and exact error strings are in llms-full.txt §2–3. The
Millennium-line verbs below are **gigi-native observables** that read /
restage geometric signatures documented in Davis Geometric — evidence in
that framework, not proofs of the Clay problems.

- Millennium observables (evidence, not proofs): `SPECTRAL_GAUGE ... MODE MAGNETIC` builds a complex Hermitian magnetic Laplacian whose level-spacing statistics sit in the GUE class (measured spacing-ratio r̃ ≈ 0.605, GUE anchor 0.5996) vs the real cos-weight Laplacian's GOE (measured r̃ ≈ 0.527, GOE anchor 0.5307) — a gauge-native readout that restages a Riemann-line signature; `SPECTRAL ... MODE MATRIX` returns the raw signed symmetric spectrum with an `instability_fraction` (P-vs-NP observable); `HOLONOMY ... AROUND CYCLE` walks a closed loop and returns a quaternion + `order_estimate` reading the lens-space π₁=ℤ/p class (Poincaré observable)
- Dense spectrum controls: `SPECTRAL_GAUGE ... FULL [LIMIT k]` (ascending real spectrum, dense, V ≤ 4096 default), `... MODE MAGNETIC BULK k [AROUND σ | IN [a,b]]` (the k centermost interior eigenvalues); `GIGI_DENSE_CEIL` env opts the dense ceiling up into the clamped band [4096, 8192] (raise-only, machine-safety knob). Beyond the ceiling a typed `SparseUnavailable` names Phase 2.1 — the sparse interior arm is **not shipped** (deferred)
- Gauge-field flux init: `GAUGE_FIELD f GROUP U(1) INIT FLUX RANDOM SEED n ON LATTICE l` / `INIT FLUX UNIFORM φ` — seeded deterministic per-edge U(1) phases (materializes a theta bundle); clauses in any order
- Marcella EXPLAIN family: `EXPLAIN SECTION b AT id VECTOR (v0..v383)` (per-vector κ_v = |1−cos(v,μ)|/R_cos rows, additive, excluded from record_kappa), `EXPLAIN SECTION b AT <field> IN (v1,…,vn)` (grouped batch, one read-lock), typed 404 on a missing key, and on-demand Welford stats over mmap-backed bundles
- Marcella dials: `GET .../horizon` and `.../capacity` accept opt-in `fields=`, `locus=<field>=<value>`, `k=` scoping (statistics through the same formulas, over a named vector family and/or k-NN neighborhood — default responses byte-identical); `POST .../windowed_coherence` returns a per-window laminar verdict (default threshold 0.91)
- Query fixes + demo bundles: `DEFINE PATTERN p AS a=1 OR b=2` now parses the AND/OR combinator chain (base-AND-(groups-ORed), COVER parity); bundle-less `EMIT` (e.g. `SHOW BUNDLES ... EMIT CSV`) now dispatches through the executor and returns the `GIGI_EMIT_DIR` gate error instead of a silent ok; `examples/seed_demo_bundles.py` seeds the public demo bundles (`stations`, `chembl`, `tetmesh_demo`) served through `/v1/public/gql` behind the `GIGI_PUBLIC_BUNDLES` allowlist
- Durability: the DHOOM snapshot encoder's computed-field detection was O(F³·N) — days of compute on wide numeric bundles (384 scalar fibers), wedging the boot snapshot. Now cached and capped at 64 candidate fields (O(F·N + F³)); high-field bundles snapshot in seconds. `.dhoom` + WAL formats are byte-unchanged, so old snapshots still open. This is a durability **fix**; heap-only bundles are still ephemeral until a whole-engine `/v1/admin/snapshot`

## Interactive demos (web extras, not in the printed book)

- [The Mesh That Audits Itself](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-mesh-that-audits-itself): a tetrahedral mesh stored as GIGI fibers — certified dihedral-angle data per tet, adaptive refinement with a queryable audit trail, obtuse-class coloring (Castillo classification), live queries against the public instance
- [The Weather on a Sphere That Notices](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-weather-on-a-sphere-that-notices): 480 streaming stations on a globe; per-insert curvature κ = |v−μ|/R prices anomalies with no monitoring job
- [Chemical Space Has a Shape](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/chemical-space-has-a-shape): 2,200 molecules in MW×logP×TPSA property space; κ finds planted outliers; COVER queries carve visible regions
- [The Spectrum Finds the Rooms](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-spectrum-finds-the-rooms): a graph laid out at its own Laplacian eigenvectors, recomputed live; the spectral gap λ₁ falls as communities disconnect
- [The Loop That Remembers](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-loop-that-remembers): honest discrete parallel transport on a curved terrain with a live Gauss–Bonnet receipt (walked deficit vs integrated curvature)
- [Noise to Everyone Else](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/noise-to-everyone-else): gauge encryption in 3D — per-letter SO(3) scrambling, key ratchet, revocation, and invariant verification without decryption

## Exercises (all 126, interactive)

- [Chapter exercise pages](https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/exercises/ch01): ch01 through ch18, one page per chapter — each has an in-browser GQL console pointed at the public instance, a workbook with local progress, and a visual instrument built on the chapter's math (see llms-full.txt for the per-chapter list)

## Live API (for agents)

- POST https://gigi-stream.fly.dev/v1/public/gql with JSON body {"query": "SHOW BUNDLES;"} — read-only, no key, CORS open; the `tetmesh_demo` bundle is populated (5,760 sections). Try {"query": "HEALTH tetmesh_demo;"} for live geometry.
